statement.markdown: Heapify an array after inserting a new element at a specific position.
statement.inputFormat: Input is an array of integers and the index at which the new element is to be inserted. The array is already a max heap. The new element is also an integer.
statement.outputFormat: The function should return the resulting heap after the insertion.
statement.notes: The inserted element should ensure the heap property is maintained. You can assume the input array has at least 1 element.

examples.explanation: Example 1: Insert 1 at index 3 of the max heap [5, 3].
Example 2: Insert 2 at index 1 of the max heap [2, 1, 0].

hints.text: Recall the heapify operation and how it maintains the heap property.

editorial.title: Heapify Insertion
editorial.intuition: Insert the new element in its correct position in the heap and then perform a heapify operation to maintain the heap property.
editorial.optimalSolution: Perform the insert operation and follow it by a max heapify operation starting from the index at which the new element was inserted.

buggyStarterCode.cpp: class Solution {public: vector<int> heapifyInsertion(vector<int> array, int n, int idx) {	for (int i = n - 1; i > idx; --i) swap(array[i], array[i - 1]);	return array;}};
buggyStarterCode.java: class Solution {public static int[] heapifyInsertion(int[] array, int n, int idx) {	for (int i = n - 1; i > idx; --i) {	int temp = array[i]; array[i] = array[i - 1]; array[i - 1] = temp;	}	return array;}};
buggyStarterCode.python:
class Solution: def heapifyInsertion(self, nums: list[int], n: int, idx: int) -> list[int]:	for i in range(n - 1, idx, -1):	nums[i], nums[i - 1] = nums[i - 1], nums[i]
	return nums;
buggyStarterCode.javascript: function heapifyInsertion(nums, n, idx) {	for (let i = n - 1; i > idx; --i) {	let temp = nums[i]; nums[i] = nums[i - 1]; nums[i - 1] = temp;	}	return nums;
